New WIC Food Options
New WIC Food Options
WIC just got even better! Explore new WIC foods available for you and your family.
See New WIC Foods
What's New?
More whole grains choices, like corn masa flour, pita bread, whole wheat bagels, whole wheat English muffins and new whole grain breakfast cereals
New yogurt flavors, such as peach, mango and strawberry banana
Canned fish (tuna, salmon, sardines) for parents and children age one and older
More plant-based milk options
Cash value benefit for fruits and vegetables (fresh, frozen or canned) instead of jarred baby food
Fresh herbs can be bought using your fruit and vegetable benefit
Remember you can use the California WIC Card with its special quick-response (QR) code to shop for seasonal, fresh fruits and vegetables at local farmers' markets
More package sizes will be allowed when buying WIC-approved foods
To see your new food choices, visit California WIC's
MyFamily website
and look for the
new Shopping Guide
. You can also use the
California WIC App
to see your food balance and scan foods as you shop.
Start shopping for your WIC foods
Find a WIC Grocer
Find a Farmers' Market

FAQs
▾
Why is WIC changing the foods?
WIC is updating foods to give families more choices, healthier options and foods that fit different cultures and diets. The new WIC foods begin in mid-April. Your California WIC App and Shopping Guide will show the new food options when they are available.
▾
Do these changes affect all WIC families?
Yes. All California WIC families will be able to see the updated foods beginning in mid-April. Make sure to check your California WIC App as April gets closer.
▾
How can I learn more about the new foods?
You can learn more about these food updates by talking to your WIC counselor at your next appointment.
▾
How will I know what I can buy?
The California WIC App will show your updated food balance and includes a food scan feature to check if a food is WIC approved. The Shopping Guide will be made available to WIC families by mid-April and will list all new foods and sizes.
▾
What should I do if I have trouble buying the new WIC foods at the store?
It may take some time for grocery stores to fully update their systems with the new WIC foods. If you have trouble buying your food at the store, speak with the manager first. If that does not solve the issue, contact your local WIC office or call the California WIC program at
1-800-852-5770
or
[email protected]
.
▾
Will I lose any of my current foods?
You will still get healthy foods each month. Some amounts may change—like whole-grains—but you will also receive more choices and flexibility, including new foods.
